citations_valid.cited_case_name: Ass'n of Mexican-Am. Educators v. State of California
Escriba v. Foster Poultry Farms, Inc.

citations_valid.parenthetical: denying costs where "the fatal shooting of [decedent] by a police officer during a traffic stop undoubtedly raises issues of substantial public importance"
declining costs, finding that a "claim of use of excessive force by a law enforcement officer in the context of [plaintiff's] arrest is an issue of substantial public importance"
[I]mposing the cost bill on Plaintiff would lead to a harsh result that could chill individual litigants of modest means seeking to vindicate important rights under the civil rights laws.

citations_valid.cited_case_name: Stanley v. Univ. of S. California
citations_valid.parenthetical: Without civil rights litigants who are willing to test the boundaries of our laws, we would not have made much of the progress that has occurred in this nation since [Brown v. Board of Education].

citations_valid.cited_case_name: Hunter v. City & Cnty. of San Francisco
citations_valid.parenthetical: finding a chilling effect on future civil rights litigation where the plaintiff's "claim of excessive force was significant"

citations_valid.cited_case_name: Hunter v. City & Cnty. of San Francisco
citations_valid.parenthetical: [T]here exists a significant economic disparity between Plaintiff and the City and County of San Francisco.
finding that the County of Sonoma and its police officers, as "public entities and employees, have significantly more resources" than the indigent plaintiffs

finding for the plaintiffs on this factor after they lost on summary judgment, reasoning that the issues "involved consideration of complex questions of law and fact" and the plaintiff's claims "had support and were not frivolous"
finding that the issues presented in a civil rights issue were close and difficult because "[r]esolution of [the] issues was not obvious" and the case was "vigorously litigated"
